title_original: IQM and Zurich Instruments launch real-time quantum error correction demonstrator with NVIDIA NVQLink - IQM Quantum Computers
summary_original: IQM Quantum Computers and Zurich Instruments have partnered to develop a real-time quantum error correction demonstrator using IQM’s 20-qubit superconducting processor, the Zurich Instruments ZQCS Quantum Control System, and NVIDIA NVQLink for GPU-accelerated classical computing. Announced on March 16, 2026, the project is positioned as a foundational step toward scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computers suitable for enterprise and datacenter deployment. The demonstrator achieves closed-loop, low-latency decoding and feedback, which are key capabilities for operating logical qubits at scale. CEO Jan Goetz of IQM emphasized building infrastructure for widely distributed quantum systems, while Zurich Instruments’ CEO Andrea Orzati highlighted the integration with supercomputing infrastructure as a milestone for reliable, operational quantum error correction.
